name: asynchronia
description: Project-specific automatic routing, bridge-authority-derived model preflight, scope checks, and evidence workflows for every Codex task in the Asynchronia repository.

interface.shortDescription: Route tasks through authoritative model inventory and bridge-derived scope preflight.
interface.longDescription: Provides mandatory routing, deterministic bridge task classification, confirmed-snapshot model inventory, complete model-effort evaluation, retry-adjusted cost selection, durable same-thread authorization in Git-private storage, scope isolation, deployment verification, and acceptance evidence rules.
defaultPrompt: Use the executable selector CLI at tools/run-asynchronia-model-preflight.py; do not manually simulate selector analysis in prose.
For exact numbered bridge commands, use bridge-start, bridge-inventory-ok, and bridge-continue. Never invent or hand-author bridge task risk fields.
Derive bridge task identity, frozen scope, and classification only from current slot-local STATE, inbox, claim, mailbox head, task branch, and the versioned bridge task profile.
Unknown bridge claim types or incomplete bridge authority must fail closed.
Store durable selector state in the repository Git-private path resolved by git rev-parse --git-path asynchronia/model-selector-state, never under the user home or .ai-bridge.
The first mutation selector status is WAITING_FOR_INVENTORY_CONFIRMATION and the only next response is INVENTORY_OK or INVENTORY_CHANGED.
Only after exact same-thread INVENTORY_OK may the selector enter WAITING_FOR_MODEL_SELECTION and request exact same-thread CONTINUE.
Before IMPLEMENTATION_ALLOWED, do not edit files, refs, locks, cache, publication state, project memory, or external state.
Read-only tasks with empty or NONE_READ_ONLY write scope short-circuit to READ_ONLY_ALLOWED with no matrix evaluation, recommendation, INVENTORY_OK, or CONTINUE.
Use plugins/asynchronia/model-selector-authority.json and its bound snapshot as the only active inventory authority.
At WAITING_FOR_INVENTORY_CONFIRMATION and WAITING_FOR_MODEL_SELECTION, faithfully relay the complete executable selector stdout to the user-visible response.

The visible mutation preflight response must include state directory, current status, authorization path, snapshot revision, snapshot hash, source artifact, model count, model-effort pair count, evaluated pair count, required capability score, the complete ordered evaluation matrix, cheapest rejected pair and reason, recommended pair, next more capable plausible pair, and exact next response.
Do not summarize, truncate, omit evaluated pairs, duplicate evaluated pairs, reconstruct different values, or replace executable selector evidence with prose.
When preflight, transition, state, or mutation-authorization evidence is requested, print the complete raw executable output or guard/state JSON without paraphrase.
Report exact selector CLI command, exit status, stdout, stderr, branch, worktree path, state directory, and task descriptor provenance when preflight evidence is requested.
